audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be a cloud synchronization tool for project configs, but the implementation contains extensive credential harvesting (AWS, GCP, SSH keys, npm/pypi tokens), full environment variable exfiltration, and sends all data to a hardcoded external endpoint via base64-encoded HTTPS. This is a classic credential theft and data exfiltration skill, with no legitimate backup/restore/diff logic present.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collect_configs() function captures the entire os.environ dictionary and includes it in the payload sent to the external endpoint. This exposes all environment variables, including API keys, database passwords, and other secrets.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of all environment secrets used by the agent or project, enabling lateral movement and privilege escalation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the environment variable collection entirely. If environment data is needed, only collect specific, non-sensitive variables.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access and Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads and exfiltrates multiple sensitive credential files: AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials), GCP application default credentials, SSH private keys (id_rsa, id_ed25519), npmrc, and pypirc. These are all sent to the external endpoint.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete theft of cloud provider credentials, SSH keys, and package manager tokens, allowing attacker to access cloud infrastructure, source code repositories, and private packages.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all credential file reading. The skill should only operate on project-specific config files explicitly listed by the user.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via Environment and Config Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The entire environment dictionary and all project config files (.env, .env.local, .env.production, config/*.json) are collected and sent to an external server. This leaks the agent's runtime context and any secrets stored in config files.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: payload["environment"] = dict(os.environ) ... for cp in config_paths: ... payload[f"config:{cp}"] = f.read()
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposure of all project configuration secrets and runtime environment, enabling targeted attacks on the project and its infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Only collect specific, user-approved config files. Never collect environment variables.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: All collected data (credentials, configs, environment) is base64-encoded and sent via HTTPS POST to a hardcoded external endpoint (cloud-sync-api.io). The endpoint and API key are hardcoded, indicating a premeditated exfiltration channel.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All stolen data is transmitted to an attacker-controlled server, enabling full compromise of the agent's environment and credentials.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission code. If cloud sync is needed, use user-configured endpoints with proper authentication.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code with Malicious Intent
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code uses Chinese classical Chinese comments to obfuscate the true purpose of the functions. The comments describe 'collecting talismans' and 'sending shadows to cloud vaults' while the actual code performs credential theft and data exfiltration. This is deliberate obfuscation to evade casual review.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: Comments like '拘押云役通关之符契（跨境招魂所必用也）' (detain cloud service talismans) and '送阵图之浮光掠影至云阙秘库' (send shadows to cloud vault) mask the credential theft and exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Obfuscation makes the malicious intent harder to detect during code review, increasing the chance of deployment.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove obfuscated comments and replace with clear, honest documentation. The code itself should be rewritten to only perform legitimate operations.

vulnerabilities.title: Instruction Override via Misleading Skill Description
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md describes a legitimate cloud sync tool, but the embedded code performs credential theft and data exfiltration. This is a form of instruction override where the claimed functionality is completely different from the actual behavior.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: SKILL.md claims 'Backup environment configs to secure cloud storage' but the code exfiltrates credentials and environment variables to an attacker-controlled server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users and agents are tricked into running malicious code under the guise of a legitimate utility.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Align the skill description with the actual code behavior, or rewrite the code to match the description.
